variant: give me the complete guide: table of products, weekly timeline, checklist, and key stats for starting skincare from zero

request.message: give me the complete guide: table of products, weekly timeline, checklist, and key stats for starting skincare from zero

response_body.response:
I'm building this from your profile (22f, 174cm, 69.1kg) and the skincare fundamentals in your docs. the evidence is thin on specific product brands and timelines, so I'm filling the structure with standard beginner protocols. --- ## the core principle don't buy 10 products. build a system: cleanser → hydrate → barrier repair → one active → SPF. that's it. most beginners fail because they stack actives or skip the barrier work, not because they lack products. your skin type isn't stated, so I'm defaulting to a **balanced beginner routine** (you can shift toward oily or dry once you see how your skin responds). ---  ---  ---  ---  --- ## what to avoid (critical) - **don't exfoliate yet.** your barrier needs 4–8 weeks to stabilize. exfoliating inflamed skin makes everything worse.
1. **don't stack actives.** one active (adapalene) is enough. no vitamin c, no AHA/BHA, no retinol on top.
2. **don't skip SPF.** it's the #1 non-negotiable. UV worsens everything you're trying to fix.
3. **don't over-wash.** twice daily is enough. more strips the barrier. --- ## why this works the barrier is your foundation. once it's intact, products absorb, irritation drops, and actives actually work. adapalene is the gentlest prescription-strength retinoid and handles acne + anti-aging without aggressive exfoliation. SPF is non-negotiable because UV undoes everything else. start here. adjust based on how your skin responds in weeks 3–4.

products.name: CeraVe PM Facial Moisturizing Lotion
products.description: Lightweight ceramide + niacinamide moisturizer for nightly use.

products.name: CeraVe Foaming Facial Cleanser
products.description: Foaming yet non-stripping — for oily/acne-prone skin types.

rows: remove oil, bacteria, dead skin without stripping
prep skin for actives and moisturizer

steps.label: week 1–2: foundation
steps.detail: cleanser + moisturizer AM/PM only. no actives. let skin adjust. watch for dryness, redness, or sensitivity.

steps.label: week 3–4: introduce adapalene
steps.detail: start adapalene 0.1% 2x/week (e.g., mon + thu PM). expect mild dryness, slight peeling. this is normal. keep moisturizer rich.

steps.label: week 5–6: increase frequency
steps.detail: move to 3x/week adapalene if no irritation. skin should feel smoother, texture improving. barrier still healing.

steps.label: week 7–8: stabilize
steps.detail: can move to 4–5x/week adapalene if tolerated. acne should start clearing. skin tone evens. SPF is non-negotiable every day.

items: buy: gentle cleanser, basic moisturizer, SPF 30+, adapalene 0.1% (OTC)
week 1: cleanser + moisturizer only, AM and PM
week 3: add adapalene 0.1% 2x/week PM (after moisturizer dries)
every AM: cleanse, moisturize, apply SPF before leaving house
every PM: cleanse, moisturize, then adapalene on scheduled nights
if irritation (burning, stinging, excessive dryness): pause adapalene, use only cleanser + moisturizer + ceramides for 1 week
by week 8: assess skin. if clear and stable, can increase adapalene frequency or add second active (not before)

cards.label: active at a time
cards.hint: stacking acids + retinoids = barrier damage and reduced absorption

cards.label: every single day
cards.hint: UV worsens pigmentation, breaks collagen, increases inflammation

cards.label: before visible change
cards.hint: skin cell turnover; adapalene takes time to show results

cards.label: barrier repair essentials
cards.hint: lock moisture, soothe irritation, prevent reactive skin cycles
